name: preregistration_t1_post_closure_X_series_20260516
user_directive_quote: fix it as a 10x researcher. maximize utilization on the remote server.

motivation.evening_push_closure_findings: Three slot push 2026-05-15 evening closed negative. Data-dive on Slot D's 46 abstained subjects (50% coverage) revealed: (a) items 11/13 are NOT the source of error in hard subjects — items 9/10/12/14 are (1.10-1.19x worse); (b) abstained subjects have +1.8 years longer PD duration (kept 6.5 vs abst 8.3); (c) top-3 hardest subjects (NLS121, NLS187, NLS191) all have H&Y stage inconsistent with motor-exam severity; (d) disagreement score has false-flags where V2-V3 disagree but iter34 nails it.
motivation.implication: Months of effort on items 11/13 (PH/MFDFA/self-norm/replacement) targeted the wrong items. Real lift opportunities are on the abstention rule (X1) and disease-duration / per-item-9-12 architectures (X2, X3).

fwer_policy.primary_blocking_gate: replicated-uncorrected α=0.05 + MCID +0.005 + BH-FDR q<=0.10 across the 3 X probes

report_only.notes: evening push closed at n=28; X-series adds n=3 → n=31. Lifetime Bonferroni gate 0.998 structurally unreachable.

probe_X1.label: two_signal_abstention_v2v3_AND_HY_inconsistency
probe_X1.hypothesis: Combining V2-V3-GSP disagreement (Slot D canonical) with H&Y-vs-ŷ_t1 inconsistency as a second y-free signal lifts retained CCC over Slot D baseline at both 70% and 50% coverage by reducing FALSE-FLAG abstentions on subjects where V2-V3 disagree but iter34 is accurate.
probe_X1.y_free_argument: Both signals are functions of (x, trained_artifacts) only. H&Y is a clinical feature input column from pd_demographic_clinical_v1.csv (NOT a target). H&Y-to-T1 scaling is via fold-local linear regression on training-fold (H&Y, y_t1) — no test-fold y used.

design.signal_2: s_hy(x) = |hy_implied_t1(x) - yhat_iter34(x)| where hy_implied_t1(x) = a*HY(x) + b, (a,b) from train-fold OLS regression of y_t1 on H&Y
design.combined_score: z-score normalize each signal on train fold; s_combined(x) = max(z_disagree(x), z_hy(x)) — abstain on subjects with high score on EITHER signal
design.comparison_baseline: Slot D (V2-V3 + item-13 PH correction) retained CCC at same coverage

predicted_delta_ccc.reasoning: Catching ~2-3 false-flag subjects per coverage and replacing with true-hard subjects should lift retained CCC by +0.01-0.03

probe_X1.kill_threshold: retained CCC delta < 0 OR ANY 5-null gate fails OR sanity-y-nan fails
probe_X1.compute: master-local ~1 min (no LGB, just normalization + Ridge for item-13 correction reuse)

probe_X2.label: disease_duration_stratified_stage2_affine
probe_X2.hypothesis: Stratifying training-fold subjects into short-duration (<7 yr PD) and long-duration (>=7 yr PD), fitting a per-stratum affine correction (slope, intercept) on iter34's t1_sum_pred, then applying the stratum-matched correction to the held-out subject lifts T1_sum LOOCV CCC over iter34 baseline by >= +0.005.
probe_X2.mechanism_rationale: Data shows +1.8 yr PD duration shift between kept and abstained subjects. Hypothesis: long-duration PD develops different motor signatures (more axial/freezing, less tremor) that iter34's chain doesn't calibrate optimally for both subgroups simultaneously. Affine correction per stratum captures the systematic over/under-prediction bias per group.
probe_X2.wall_orthogonality: Distinct from F59 (Stage-1 widening with clinical extras failed): we don't add clinical features to Stage-1; we add a Stage-2 STRATUM-CONDITIONAL CALIBRATION. Distinct from F61 (tail-aware retraining): we stratify by clinical input (duration), not by residual magnitude. Distinct from F58 (1-param blend): we have 2-param per stratum (slope+intercept) trained on outer-train.
probe_X2.y_free_at_fit: Stratum membership for held-out subject is determined by their (known) PD duration. Within each stratum, slope+intercept fit on training-fold (yhat_iter34, y_t1) pairs — no test-fold y used.

design.threshold_provenance: median of kept-abstained PD-duration distribution (6.5 + 8.3) / 2 = 7.4; rounded to 7.0; predeclared NOT tuned
design.stratum_model: least-squares affine y_t1 ~ a * yhat_iter34 + b within stratum on training fold
design.fallback_if_stratum_too_small: if either stratum has < 15 training subjects, use the full-fold affine (no stratification) for that fold
design.missing_pd_duration_handling: subjects with NaN years_since_pd assigned to long-duration stratum (conservative, matches WPD subjects who tend to be longer-disease)

predicted_delta_ccc.reasoning: Per-stratum N=~45 is small; affine has only 2 params per stratum so overfitting risk is bounded; expected modest lift if duration signal is real

probe_X2.kill_threshold: 5-fold Δ̄ < +0.000 OR D4 audit ΔMAE > 0 with corr(correction, sum_resid) <= 0 OR ANY 5-null fails
probe_X2.compute: remote ~3-5 min (LGB-free; pure numpy/sklearn affine fits)

probe_X3.label: items_9_12_transient_targeted_residual_correction
probe_X3.hypothesis: A joint item_only Ridge correction on items 9 (chair rise) AND 12 (postural stability) residuals using available phase-locked / TUG-localized features lifts T1_sum LOOCV CCC by >= +0.005 vs iter34, with D4 audit clean. These are the two items where abstained subjects' residual errors are 1.18-1.19x worse.
probe_X3.mechanism_rationale: Items 9 and 12 are TRANSIENT sub-second events (chair-rise impulse, pull-test recovery). V2 windowed-spectral features average over full task duration, missing transient signatures. Phase-locked features should capture transient signatures specifically.
probe_X3.wall_orthogonality: Distinct from F36-D (phase-locked feature injection INTO iter34's V2 K=500 pool — failed): we use item_only correction architecture (per F-hypothesis-restricted-bypasses-K500). Distinct from F35-C (replacement-LGB for items 9/12 — failed because iter34's chain has signal for items 9 CCC=0.234, 12 CCC=0.566): we CORRECT not REPLACE. Distinct from S5/S8 (item-13 PH/MFDFA): different items, different features.

design.cache_source: cache_phaselocked_item9.csv if present (else cache_item9_event_moment.csv); cache_phaselocked_item12.csv if present (else cache_item_specific_features.csv item-12 columns); cache_nonlinear_dynamics item-12 RQA features
design.architecture: for each of items 9 and 12 separately: fold-local Ridge α-grid {10, 30, 100, 300, 1000} via inner 5-fold OOF residual CCC; corrected per-item = iter34.item_X_pred + λ*Ridge_α*(prediction). λ=1.0 fixed (per evening push tri-CLI synthesis). Then T1_sum_corrected = iter34.t1_sum_pred + correction_9 + correction_12

predicted_delta_ccc.reasoning: Slot A/B'/C all FAIL on item-13 correction at +/-0.005 magnitude. Items 9 and 12 may behave similarly. Expected sub-MCID directional positive at best.

probe_X3.kill_threshold: 5-fold Δ̄ < +0.000 OR D4 corr(correction, sum_resid) <= 0 on either item OR ΔMAE > 0 OR ANY 5-null fails

execution_plan.X2: remote slave (parallel with X3), via gpu.sh
execution_plan.X3: remote slave (parallel with X2), via gpu.sh
execution_plan.audit: aggregate all 3 in single audit table when all complete
execution_plan.five_null_gate_per_probe: scrambled_y, sid_shuffle, canary_noise (for X3), transductive, sanity_y_nan — same pattern as evening push

do_not: Use test-fold y at fit time in ANY probe (Law #9).
Tune stratification threshold post-hoc (X2 predeclared at 7.0).
Substitute alphas/lambdas/cache sources after seeing intermediate results.
Extend X1/X2/X3 into iter-X.5 if they fail.
